subject: Physics
chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

formulas.name: Wave-particle duality
formulas.formula: Wave-like behavior: λ = h/p; Particle-like behavior: λ = h/mv
formulas.variables: λ = wavelength, h = Planck's constant, p = momentum, m = mass, v = velocity
formulas.units: m, J s, kg m/s, kg, m/s
formulas.notes: Expresses the dual nature of radiation and matter.

formulas.name: De Broglie Wavelength
formulas.variables: λ = wavelength, h = Planck's constant, p = momentum
formulas.notes: Calculates the wavelength of a particle.

formulas.name: Uncertainty Principle
formulas.variables: Δx = uncertainty in position, Δp = uncertainty in momentum, h = Planck's constant
formulas.notes: States that it's impossible to know both position and momentum of a particle simultaneously.

formulas.name: Compton Scattering
formulas.variables: Δλ = change in wavelength, λ' = scattered wavelength, λ = incident wavelength, h = Planck's constant, m = electron mass, c = speed of light, θ = scattering angle
formulas.units: m, J s, kg, m/s, rad
formulas.notes: Calculates the change in wavelength of a photon after Compton scattering.

formulas.name: Einstein's Photoelectric Equation
formulas.variables: K = maximum kinetic energy, h = Planck's constant, v = frequency of incident light, φ = work function of the material
formulas.notes: Describes the relationship between the energy of incident light and the kinetic energy of emitted electrons.

formulas.name: Photoelectric Effect
formulas.variables: K = maximum kinetic energy, h = Planck's constant, f = frequency of incident light, φ = work function of the material
formulas.notes: States that the energy of emitted electrons depends on the frequency of incident light.
